title: RPL DODAGVersionNumber: Python plain mod-256 vs Rust/C RFC 6550 lollipop - rejoin divergence
description: python/src/lichen/rpl/dodag.py:42-52 implements plain modular arithmetic (diff=(new-old)%256; return 0 < diff < 128); its docstring CLAIMS RFC 6550 Section 7.2 lollipop semantics but does not implement them. Rust (rust/lichen-rpl/src/dodag.rs:32-48) and C (lichen/subsys/lichen/rpl/dodag.c:36-59) implement the real lollipop: linear region 0-127 (simple compare), circular region 128-255 (mod-128, window 16), linear always newer than circular. Concrete divergent verdicts: new=200,old=100 -> Python newer, Rust/C not newer; new=200,old=150 -> Python newer, Rust/C not (diff 50 > 16); new=100,old=200 -> Python not newer, Rust/C newer. Any version transition crossing the 127/128 boundary makes the Python oracle disagree with both compiled stacks on whether to rejoin the DODAG - corrupts any Python-driven cross-impl test of version handling. Fix Python to the lollipop (Rust/C are the reference); add cross-region version vectors to test/vectors/rpl_messages.json.
